viewer_unsupported_format: Unsupported file format — can't preview or edit “{name}”
empty_dropzone_title: Drop a folder/file here or click to open
empty_select_file_hint: Choose an .adoc file from the sidebar to preview it.
shortcuts_command_palette: Command Palette — every action in one place
shortcuts_go_to_heading: Go to Heading — jump in the current document
shortcuts_find_in_files: Find in Files — search across the workspace
shortcuts_new_tab: New tab in the active pane
shortcuts_find_in_doc: Find inside the current preview / editor
shortcut_file_save: Save (auto-save runs 1s after the last edit)
shortcut_nav_quick_open: Quick Open: jump to a file by name
shortcut_nav_go_to_symbol: Go to Symbol: jump to a heading in the current file
shortcut_nav_find_in_files: Find in Files: search content across the workspace
shortcut_nav_workspace_symbols: Go to Symbol in Workspace: jump to a heading across all docs
shortcut_view_split_editor: Split editor (toggle): open a second pane side by side
shortcut_view_reader_mode: Toggle Reader / Zen mode (hide chrome, center preview)
toc_no_headings: No headings found in this document.
update_available_no_notes: No release notes were published with this update.
update_up_to_date: You are on the latest version of AsciiMark.
release_notes_no_notes: No release notes were published for this version.
about_tagline: AsciiDoc and Markdown viewer with diagrams, math, and live preview.
backlinks_empty: No other documents reference this file.
ai_empty_body: Chat about the document you're editing. Configure a provider to go live.
ai_empty_no_provider_body: Add a provider and model in Settings to start chatting.
ai_error_generic: Something went wrong. Please try again.
settings_ai_no_models: No models — load or pick a provider
settings_indexing_off_desc: No background indexing. AI uses only the active document. Inline actions still work.
settings_indexing_lite_desc: Fast keyword index over every .md/.adoc. Cites docs by exact-term match. Zero model downloads.
settings_indexing_full_desc: Adds vector embeddings + reranker. Best answers; downloads a model on first activation.
settings_mcp_empty: Connect MCP servers to give the assistant tools (search, memory, etc.).
settings_privacy_body: AsciiMark is local-first with zero telemetry. AI provider keys live only in the OS keychain, never in plaintext config.
settings_placeholder: These settings are managed in the toolbar for now.
site_hero_title: Ship docs faster with a local-first preview workflow.
site_hero_description: AsciiMark keeps authoring and preview side by side across desktop and browser. Install the latest build from GitHub Releases and keep everything in one ecosystem.
site_hero_caption: Desktop preview with sidebar, tabs, and table of contents.
site_hero_alt: AsciiMark desktop app with Markdown file, sidebar tree, and table of contents
site_features_more_description: Themes, PDF export, the Chrome extension, the full keyboard map, and more. See the guide for screenshots and details.
site_feature_formats_description: Renders both formats with admonitions, includes, frontmatter, and reusable partials in a single viewer.
site_feature_panes_description: Open files side by side. Single-click opens preview tabs (italic), edit or double-click pins them — VSCode-style.
site_feature_keyboard_description: Quick Open, Command Palette, Go-to-Heading, Workspace Symbols, and Find-in-Files — all one shortcut away.
site_feature_backlinks_description: The right gutter has a References tab listing every doc in the workspace that links to the active file.
site_feature_symbols_description: Cmd/Ctrl+Alt+O fuzzy-matches headings across every doc in the workspace and jumps to the right line.
site_feature_reader_description: Cmd/Ctrl+. collapses the chrome and centers the preview at a comfortable reading width. Toggle off the same way.
site_feature_diagrams_description: Mermaid, PlantUML, Graphviz, and KaTeX render inside the preview without extra setup.
site_feature_multiroot_description: Pin several folders into the sidebar at once — handy when reading docs spread across repos.
site_feature_localfirst_description: Files stay on your machine; signed releases land via the auto-updater on startup.
site_downloads_subtitle: All links below point to releases/latest/download in the public repository.
site_notice_macos: macOS may block unsigned apps on first launch. Run {cmd} after installing.
site_notice_windows: Windows SmartScreen may require More info and then Run anyway.
site_chrome_ext_description: AsciiMark is also a Chrome extension — render .adoc and .md files directly in any tab without installing the desktop app.
privacy_data_collection_p: AsciiMark does not collect analytics, trackers, or telemetry, and does not send personal data to any AsciiMark-controlled server. There is no AsciiMark backend.
privacy_scope_p: This policy applies to both the Chrome extension and the desktop app.
privacy_local_processing_p: Document parsing and rendering happen locally inside your browser tab (extension) or inside the Tauri webview (desktop app). Document text is never uploaded by AsciiMark infrastructure. The only exceptions are explicit, user-triggered network requests listed below.
privacy_local_storage_p: AsciiMark persists user-controlled state in the browser's localStorage and, on the extension, in IndexedDB and chrome.storage.session. Nothing in this list leaves your machine.
privacy_local_storage_item_editor: Editor preferences (indent mode, line numbers, wrap, invisibles, font).
privacy_local_storage_item_recent: Recent files and recent folders, plus pinned favorites.
privacy_local_storage_item_tabs: Tab session per pane (open files and the active tab) and the pane layout (number of panes, active pane, split ratio).
privacy_local_storage_item_layout: Sidebar width and visibility, table-of-contents visibility and depth.
privacy_local_storage_item_dir_handles: Extension only: directory handles in IndexedDB for session restore.
privacy_local_storage_item_url_payload: Extension only: temporary URL-mode payload in chrome.storage.session — captured by the content script when redirecting to the viewer page, cleared on session end.
privacy_permission_storage: storage (extension): used for the temporary URL-mode payload above via chrome.storage.session.
privacy_permission_kroki: host_permissions: https://kroki.io/* (extension): the only host the extension can fetch. Used solely to render Kroki diagram blocks.
privacy_permission_file_url: File URL access (optional, extension): enables previewing local file:// documents. Off by default; toggled at chrome://extensions.
privacy_network_document_urls: Document URLs (extension): when the extension renders a remote .adoc or .md URL, it fetches the file from that URL the same way a browser would — directly between your machine and the document host.
privacy_network_kroki: Kroki rendering: diagram blocks (plantuml, graphviz, mermaid, etc.) are sent as plain text to https://kroki.io via POST and the response is a static SVG. No JavaScript is fetched. Skipped if the document has no diagram blocks.
privacy_network_updater: Auto-updater (desktop): on startup the desktop app checks https://github.com/djalmajr/asciimark/releases/latest/download/latest.json for a newer version. If you accept an update prompt, the signed binary is downloaded from the same public GitHub repository. The check sends no document content — it only contacts GitHub.
privacy_sharing_p: When you copy the source URL of a document being viewed in the extension, AsciiMark strips query parameters (such as GitHub's short-lived ?token=… on raw private-repo URLs) before placing the URL on the clipboard. This prevents the token from leaking when the URL is pasted into chats or issues. The original URL the viewer uses is unchanged.
privacy_libraries_p: AsciiMark bundles open-source packages, all running locally:
privacy_library_mermaid: Mermaid (diagrams rendered locally; some Kroki diagrams use the service above)
privacy_service_kroki: Kroki (https://kroki.io) — only when your document contains supported Kroki diagram blocks.
privacy_service_github: GitHub (github.com/djalmajr/asciimark) — only for desktop auto-updates and downloads of installer / update artifacts.
privacy_changes_p: Any future updates to this policy are published on this page. The Last updated date at the top reflects the most recent change.
privacy_contact_p: For privacy questions, open an issue at github.com/djalmajr/asciimark/issues.
guide_intro_p: Everything you need to install, configure, and use AsciiMark across desktop and extension workflows.
guide_installation_chrome_p: The Chrome Extension is available on the Chrome Web Store: install AsciiMark to render .adoc and .md files in-browser with formatted preview.
guide_installation_desktop_p: Download the installer for your platform from the Home page downloads section.
guide_installation_macos_p: Since the app is not notarized, macOS may block first launch. Run once:
guide_installation_windows_p: If SmartScreen appears, click More info and then Run anyway.
guide_installation_linux_p: Make the downloaded AppImage executable before running:
guide_installation_linux_deb: The .deb package can be installed with your distro package manager.
guide_opening_folder_p: Select a local directory to browse all supported files in a tree sidebar.
guide_opening_folder_caption: Loaded folder tree in the left sidebar.
guide_opening_folder_alt: Sidebar file tree after loading a folder
guide_opening_dnd_p: Drop a folder to populate the tree, or drop a file to open it directly.
guide_opening_gitignore_p: Toggle Respect .gitignore in the file-tree visibility dropdown (slider icon) to hide every path matched by the workspace's .gitignore — including nested .gitignore files inside subdirectories. The toggle ships on by default so build artifacts (node_modules, dist, target, etc.) stay out of the tree without configuration. Flip it off to reveal everything the always-ignored folders block still hides.
guide_opening_gitignore_caption: File-tree visibility dropdown with the Respect .gitignore toggle.
guide_opening_gitignore_alt: File tree dropdown showing the Respect .gitignore toggle
guide_opening_url_p: URLs ending with .adoc and .md can be rendered directly.
guide_opening_file_url_item3: Enable Allow access to file URLs
guide_media_intro_p: AsciiMark opens images and PDFs in a builtin viewer — no external app, no leaving the workspace. Enable “Show all files” in the file-tree visibility dropdown to see them in the tree.
guide_media_images_p: Renders .png, .jpg, .jpeg, .gif, .webp, .avif, .bmp, .svg and .ico with zoom in/out, fit-to-window, and a checkerboard backdrop for transparency.
guide_media_pdf_p: PDFs render every page in a single continuous scroll (no page-flipping), with zoom and fit-to-width. Rendering is bundled, so it looks identical on macOS, Windows, and Linux.
guide_media_modes_h3: Edit, Preview, and Split by file type
guide_media_modes_p: The Edit / Edit & Preview / Preview toggle adapts to what each file supports:
guide_media_modes_item_doc: Markdown & AsciiDoc — edit, split, and preview are all available.
guide_media_modes_item_media: Images & PDF — preview only; editing is disabled.
guide_media_modes_item_text: Plain text (txt, json, yaml, …) — edit only; there is no rendered preview.
guide_media_modes_item_unsupported: Unsupported files (binaries that can't be rendered or read as text) show an “unsupported format” notice.
guide_tabs_p1: Single-click in the file tree opens the file in the preview slot — the tab title shows in italic. Click another file and the preview is replaced. Double-click, drag, or just start editing to pin the tab; pinned tabs stay until you close them.
guide_tabs_p2: The invariant: at most one preview tab per pane. Middle-click and "Open in New Tab" always create a pinned tab.
guide_tabs_caption: VSCode-style tabs — pinned upright, preview italic.
guide_tabs_alt: Active workspace with one pinned tab and one preview tab in italic
guide_navigation_item1: Use the file tree to jump between documents.
guide_navigation_item2: Follow AsciiDoc xref links in rendered preview.
guide_navigation_item3: Use browser/app back and forward history.
guide_navigation_item4: Use breadcrumbs to understand current file location.
guide_navigation_caption: Example of opening a file directly from the tree.
guide_navigation_alt: Markdown file opened from sidebar tree
guide_toc_intro: The right gutter is segmented into two tabs:
guide_toc_item_summary: Summary — the rendered document's table of contents. Scroll position drives the active heading highlight.
guide_toc_item_references: References — every other workspace doc that links to the current file via Markdown link, AsciiDoc xref, or include. The count badge tells you upfront whether the active doc is referenced.
guide_toc_outro: Both tabs stay mounted regardless of which is visible — switching is instant.
guide_toc_summary_caption: Summary tab shows the document TOC; the count on References indicates inbound links.
guide_toc_references_caption: References — workspace backlinks for the active file.
guide_symbols_p: Cmd/Ctrl+Alt+O opens a fuzzy-match palette over every heading in every doc of the workspace — not just the active file. Type the heading text or the file path to scope; pick a row and the file opens, scrolled to the right line, with the TOC active highlight already on the matching item.
guide_reader_p1: Cmd/Ctrl+. (or View → Toggle Reader Mode in the Command Palette) collapses the toolbar, sidebar, TOC, and status bar — leaving only the rendered preview centered at a comfortable reading width. Press the same shortcut to come back.
guide_reader_p2: F11 is accepted as a fallback on platforms where it isn't already taken by the OS. macOS Mission Control swallows F11 by default — use the period chord there.
guide_reader_caption: Reader / Zen mode — focused preview without distractions.
guide_search_tree_p: Filter files by name as you type.
guide_search_preview_p: Press Ctrl+F to open in-document search and navigate highlighted results.
guide_editor_split_p: Edit source and preview output side by side.
guide_editor_save_item2: Changes are written back to local files when permissions are granted.
guide_appearance_caption: Appearance and export controls in the settings menu.
guide_appearance_close_behavior_p: By default, clicking the window close button (X / Cmd+W) hides AsciiMark to the system tray — the process keeps running so reopening is instant. Open the toolbar menu and pick Window → Quit app to make the close gesture actually terminate the process. Cmd+Q always quits regardless of this setting.
guide_appearance_close_behavior_caption: Window submenu with Minimize to tray and Quit app options.
guide_appearance_close_behavior_alt: Toolbar menu showing the Window submenu with close-behavior options
guide_processing_asciidoc_p: Uses Asciidoctor with support for admonitions, tables, source blocks, xrefs, and recursive includes.
guide_processing_md_p: Uses markdown-it with plugins for task lists, footnotes, definition lists, emoji, alerts, advanced tables, and more.
guide_diagrams_item1: Mermaid diagrams render directly in preview.
guide_diagrams_item2: Kroki is used for PlantUML, Ditaa, Graphviz and related formats.
guide_export_item2: Direct PDF generation is available in supported modes.
guide_shortcuts_intro: On macOS use Cmd; on Linux and Windows use Ctrl. Press Cmd/Ctrl+/ anywhere in the app to open the live shortcuts reference.
guide_shortcut_quick_open: Quick Open — fuzzy-find a file across all open workspaces
guide_shortcut_command_palette: Command Palette — every action in one place
guide_shortcut_go_heading: Go to Heading — jump anywhere in the current document
guide_shortcut_workspace_symbols: Go to Symbol in Workspace — fuzzy-match headings across every doc
guide_shortcut_find_in_files: Find in Files — search across the workspace
guide_shortcut_reader_mode: Toggle Reader / Zen mode (chrome-less, centered preview)
guide_shortcut_new_tab: New tab in the active pane
guide_shortcut_save: Save the active document (when permissions allow)
guide_shortcut_find: Find inside the current preview / editor
guide_screenshots_intro: Captured from the desktop app. The Chrome extension shares the same UI in a smaller window.
